# Claude Code

Claude Code is Anthropic's agentic coding tool that can read, modify, and execute code in your working directory.

Open models can be used with Claude Code through Ollama's Anthropic-compatible API, enabling you to use models such as `glm-4.7`, `qwen3-coder`, `gpt-oss`.

![Claude Code with Ollama](https://files.ollama.com/claude-code.png)

## Install

Install [Claude Code](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview):

<CodeGroup>
  ```shell macOS / Linux theme={"system"}
  curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
  ```

  ```powershell Windows theme={"system"}
  irm https://claude.ai/install.ps1 | iex
  ```
</CodeGroup>

## Usage with Ollama

### Quick setup

```shell  theme={"system"}
ollama launch claude
```

To configure without launching:

```shell  theme={"system"}
ollama launch claude --config
```

### Manual setup

Claude Code connects to Ollama using the Anthropic-compatible API.

1. Set the environment variables:

```shell  theme={"system"}
export ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=ollama
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=""
export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434
```

2. Run Claude Code with an Ollama model:

```shell  theme={"system"}
claude --model gpt-oss:20b
```

Or run with environment variables inline:

```shell  theme={"system"}
ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=ollama ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434 ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="" claude --model qwen3-coder 
```

**Note:** Claude Code requires a large context window. We recommend at least 64k tokens. See the [context length documentation](/context-length) for how to adjust context length in Ollama.

## Recommended Models

* `qwen3-coder`
* `glm-4.7`
* `gpt-oss:20b`
* `gpt-oss:120b`

Cloud models are also available at [ollama.com/search?c=cloud](https://ollama.com/search?c=cloud).

<summary>Tool use: **WebFetch**</summary>
 `https://docs.ollama.com/llms.txt`

```text
# Ollama Documentation Summary

The page lists comprehensive documentation for Ollama, organized into several categories:

**API Reference Documents:**
The site includes guides for core operations like retrieving version information, displaying model specifics, and generating chat messages. Additional endpoints cover model management (copy, create, delete, pull, push), embeddings generation, and error handling. The docs note that Ollama supports "OpenAI compatibility" and "Anthropic compatibility" modes.

**Capabilities:**
Advanced features documented include embeddings for semantic search, streaming responses, structured outputs, thinking processes, tool calling, vision capabilities, and web search functionality.

**Platform & Setup Guides:**
Installation instructions are provided for Linux, macOS, and Windows. The documentation also covers Docker deployment, hardware GPU support, and context length configuration.

**Integration Support:**
The site lists integrations with numerous development tools including VS Code, JetBrains IDEs, Xcode, and specialized code editors like Cline, Roo Code, and Zed.

**Additional Resources:**
The documentation includes a CLI reference, cloud information, model importing instructions, FAQ section, troubleshooting guide, and a Modelfile reference for creating custom models.

An OpenAPI specification (openapi.yaml) is available for programmatic reference.
```
</details></tool-use>

# Claude Code

Open models can be used with Claude Code through Ollama's Anthropic-compatible API, enabling you to use models such as `glm-4.7`, `qwen3-coder`, `gpt-oss`.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 17:55:05Z)**_

The pull is running in the background (qwen3-coder is ~7-8GB). Here's what was implemented:

**Installed & configured:**
1. **Ollama 0.17.7** — via Homebrew, running as a background service at `http://localhost:11434`
2. **Environment vars** — added to `~/.zshrc`:
   ```
   ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=ollama
   ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=""
   ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434
   ```
3. **`qwen3-coder` pull** — running in background (recommended model from the docs)

**To use once the model downloads:**
```shell
# Open a new terminal (or source ~/.zshrc), then:
claude --model qwen3-coder
```

**To check pull progress:**
```shell
ollama list
```

**To switch back to Anthropic API,** unset the three env vars or open a shell without sourcing `~/.zshrc`.

Superman — the `qwen3-coder` pull will take several minutes. Once `ollama list` shows the model, you can launch Claude Code with it.

_**User (2026-03-09 17:55:57Z)**_

Which is best:
Recommended Models
qwen3-coder
glm-4.7
gpt-oss:20b
gpt-oss:120b

---

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 17:56:10Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is asking which of the recommended Ollama models is best for use with Claude Code. Let me give a direct, concise comparison based on what I know about these models.

- **qwen3-coder**: Alibaba's Qwen3 coding-specialized model. Very strong at code tasks, good context window support. Available in various sizes.
- **glm-4.7**: ChatGLM 4.7 from Zhipu AI. General purpose, multilingual (strong Chinese/English). Less coding-focused than qwen3-coder.
- **gpt-oss:20b**: This appears to be an open-source GPT-compatible model at 20B parameters. 

The larger 120B variant requires substantial hardware resources but delivers higher quality, while the 20B version strikes a reasonable balance between capability and resource demands. For agentic coding work specifically, qwen3-coder is purpose-built for the task with strong reasoning abilities, though the 120B model would be the top choice if you have the hardware to support it.

Since they're on macOS, I should really understand their system constraints first—RAM availability is the deciding factor here for which model would actually run well on their machine.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 17:56:13Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Check RAM and chip

`system_profiler SPHardwareDataType 2>/dev/null | grep -E "Memory:|Chip:|Processor"`

```text
      Chip: Apple M2
      Memory: 16 GB
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 17:56:22Z)**_

With **16GB M2**, here's the ranking for your hardware:

| Model | Size | Fits in 16GB? | Best for |
|-------|------|---------------|----------|
| **`qwen3-coder`** | ~5GB (7B) | ✅ Comfortably | **Best choice** — purpose-built for code, fast on M2 |
| `gpt-oss:20b` | ~12GB | ✅ Tight | General coding, slower, less headroom for OS |
| `glm-4.7` | ~5GB (7B) | ✅ Comfortably | Multilingual (Chinese/English), weaker at code |
| `gpt-oss:120b` | ~70GB | ❌ Won't fit | Needs 80GB+ RAM — not viable |

**Recommendation: `qwen3-coder`**

- Designed specifically for agentic coding tasks (exactly what Claude Code does)
- Fits comfortably — leaves headroom for macOS + your dev tools
- Best code quality per GB on M2 unified memory
- Already pulling in the background

`gpt-oss:120b` is off the table entirely. `gpt-oss:20b` is a fallback if `qwen3-coder` disappoints, but it'll be noticeably slower and leave only ~4GB for the OS.

ollama list
```
When `qwen3-coder` appears, you're ready.

**Step 2 — Reload your shell** (picks up the env vars we added to `.zshrc`)
```shell
source ~/.zshrc
```

**Step 3 — Verify the env vars are set**
```shell
echo $ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL   # should print: http://localhost:11434
echo $ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN  # should print: ollama
```

**Step 4 — Launch Claude Code with the model**
```shell
claude --model qwen3-coder
```

**Step 5 — Test it works**
Ask it something simple first: *"what model are you?"* or *"write a hello world in TypeScript"*

---

**One gotcha:** The env vars in `.zshrc` will apply to **all** Claude Code sessions in new terminals. If you ever want to switch back to the Anthropic API temporarily, run:
```shell
unset ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
```

---

```md
# Ollama + Claude Code Setup

Run Claude Code locally using Ollama's Anthropic-compatible API instead of the Anthropic cloud API.

## Prerequisites

- macOS with Homebrew
- Claude Code installed (`curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash`)

## Hardware Requirements

| Model | RAM Required | Notes |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| `qwen3-coder` | ~6GB | **Recommended** — coding-specialized |
| `gpt-oss:20b` | ~12GB | General coding, slower |
| `glm-4.7` | ~6GB | Multilingual, weaker at code |
| `gpt-oss:120b` | ~70GB | Requires 80GB+ RAM |

For a 16GB M2 MacBook, use `qwen3-coder`.

## Installation

### 1. Install Ollama

\```shell
brew install ollama
\```

### 2. Start the Ollama service

\```shell
brew services start ollama
\```

Verify it's running:
\```shell
curl http://localhost:11434/api/version
# Expected: {"version":"0.x.x"}
\```

### 3. Pull a model

\```shell
ollama pull qwen3-coder
\```

Check progress / confirm download complete:
\```shell
ollama list
\```

### 4. Set environment variables

Add to `~/.zshrc` (or `~/.bashrc`):

\```shell
# Ollama — Claude Code compatibility
export ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=ollama
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=""
export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434
\```

Reload your shell:
\```shell
source ~/.zshrc
\```

Verify:
\```shell
echo $ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL    # http://localhost:11434
echo $ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN  # ollama
\```

## Usage

\```shell
claude --model qwen3-coder
\```

Or inline (without modifying `.zshrc`):

\```shell
ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=ollama ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434 ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="" claude --model qwen3-coder
\```

## Switching Back to Anthropic API

To temporarily revert to the cloud API in the current terminal session:

\```shell
unset ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
\```

To make it permanent, remove the three `export` lines from `~/.zshrc`.

## Context Length

Claude Code requires a large context window — at least 64k tokens. To configure context length in Ollama, see: https://docs.ollama.com/context-length

## Troubleshooting

| Problem | Fix |
|---------|-----|
| `connection refused` on port 11434 | `brew services restart ollama` |
| Model not found | `ollama pull qwen3-coder` |
| Slow responses | Reduce other RAM usage; close browsers/apps |
| Out of memory crash | Switch to a smaller model (`qwen3-coder` over `gpt-oss:20b`) |

```

```text
File created successfully at: /Users/shivpat/harmony-forge-redesign/docs/ollama-claude-code-setup.md
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 18:06:21Z)**_

Created [docs/ollama-claude-code-setup.md](docs/ollama-claude-code-setup.md) — covers installation, hardware requirements, usage, switching back to Anthropic, and a troubleshooting table.

_**User (2026-03-09 18:06:55Z)**_

So does this mean I have access to unlimited tokens?

---

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 18:06:59Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is asking if using Ollama with Claude Code means they have access to unlimited tokens. Let me give an accurate answer.

The answer is nuanced:
- **Cost**: Yes, it's free/unlimited in terms of API cost since it's running locally
- **Context window**: No, not unlimited - it's capped by the model's context length and your RAM
- **Quality**: The local models (qwen3-coder) are significantly less capable than Claude Sonnet/Opus

Let me be direct and accurate about this.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 18:07:06Z)**_

**Cost-wise: yes. Technically: no.**

**What you get:**
- Zero API cost — runs entirely on your machine
- No rate limits, no monthly caps
- No data sent to Anthropic

**What's still limited:**
- **Context window** — `qwen3-coder` supports up to ~32k tokens by default. Your 16GB RAM is the hard ceiling. Claude Sonnet has 200k tokens.
- **Speed** — inference on M2 CPU/GPU is much slower than Anthropic's servers
- **Quality** — `qwen3-coder` (~7B params) is significantly less capable than Claude Sonnet (the model you're currently talking to). Complex reasoning, multi-file refactors, and architecture tasks will be noticeably weaker.

**Practical reality:**
Most developers use Ollama for low-stakes, repetitive tasks (autocomplete, small edits, quick lookups) and keep Anthropic API access for heavy lifting. It's not really an either/or — you can switch between them per session.

If unlimited tokens with full Claude quality is the goal, Anthropic's [Max plan](https://anthropic.com) ($100/mo) is the other option — 5x the usage limits with the actual Sonnet/Opus models.

---

_**User (2026-03-09 18:07:56Z)**_

What are other resources that allows me to have unlinited tokesn for Claude

---

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 18:08:07Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user wants to know about resources/options for unlimited tokens with Claude specifically. Let me think about what's actually available:

1. **Anthropic Max Plan** - $100/mo, 5x usage limits but not truly unlimited
2. **API with your own key** - pay per token, no limits per se but costs money
3. **AWS Bedrock** - access Claude through AWS, pay per token
4. **Google Cloud Vertex AI** - access Claude through GCP, pay per token
5. **Anthropic Claude for Teams/Enterprise** - higher limits

But none of these are truly "unlimited" in the free sense. The user seems to want free unlimited access.


The reality is there's no legitimate way to get truly free and unlimited Claude access—the paid options I mentioned are the actual tiers available, and for a free alternative with similar capabilities, I'd need to point them toward open-source models like those you can run locally with Ollama.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 18:08:14Z)**_

Honestly, there's no truly unlimited Claude — but here are the practical tiers:

**Paid Claude options (most capable):**

| Option | Cost | Limits |
|--------|------|--------|
| **Claude.ai Max** | ~$100/mo | 5x Pro limits, still soft-capped |
| **Anthropic API** | Pay-per-token | No cap, you control spend |
| **AWS Bedrock** | Pay-per-token | Enterprise scale, same models |
| **Google Vertex AI** | Pay-per-token | Same, GCP ecosystem |

**Free / effectively unlimited (not Claude):**

| Option | Quality vs Claude |
|--------|-------------------|
| **Ollama** (already set up) | Weaker, free, local |
| **Gemini 2.0 Flash** | Competitive, very generous free tier |
| **OpenRouter** | Routes to many models, has free tier |
| **Groq** | Fast inference, free tier, open models |

**The honest answer:**
If you want Claude Sonnet/Opus quality with no token anxiety, the **Anthropic API with a spending cap** is the most flexible — you pay exactly what you use, set a hard monthly limit, and there's no artificial rate cap. Heavy daily coding use typically runs $20–50/mo.

If cost is the constraint, **Gemini 2.0 Flash** via Google AI Studio is currently the strongest free-tier alternative to Claude for coding tasks.

---

_**User (2026-03-09 18:08:41Z)**_

So should I use Ollama once I run out of my Claude tokens

---

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 18:08:48Z)**_

Yes — that's exactly the right mental model.

**Use Claude (Anthropic) for:**
- Complex architecture decisions
- Multi-file refactors
- Debugging hard problems
- Anything requiring deep reasoning

**Switch to Ollama (`qwen3-coder`) when:**
- You've hit your rate limit
- Small, repetitive edits
- Boilerplate generation
- Quick lookups / simple completions

**How to switch mid-session:**

When Claude rate-limits you, open a new terminal and run:
```shell
source ~/.zshrc
claude --model qwen3-coder
```

The env vars are already set — it'll route to Ollama automatically.

**To switch back to Anthropic** (new terminal, or after rate limit resets):
```shell
unset ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
claude
```

---

One thing to know: `qwen3-coder` won't have memory of your previous Claude session — you'll need to re-explain context. Keeping a brief `CONTEXT.md` in your project (you already have several) makes the handoff much smoother.
